career.organization: American Board of Commissioners for Foreign Missions (ABCFM)
career.notes: Agent for the ABCFM after his Andover graduation.

career.location: Boston (1825-1828), then New York City (1828-1832 general agent for Mississippi Valley; 1832 onward finance secretary)
career.notes: Joined American Tract Society at its 1825 founding. In Boston 1825-1828. Transferred to New York City 1828 as general agent for the Mississippi Valley. Promoted to finance secretary 1832.

institutions.institution_blurb: American Tract Society Boston 1825-1828, then NY general agent for Mississippi Valley 1828-1832, finance secretary 1832 onward.

narrative_blocks.header: Founding-year agent then finance secretary American Tract Society
narrative_blocks.body: Joined the American Tract Society in its first year — 1825, when the New England Tract Society (1814) merged into the newly formed ATS at New York. Served at the Boston office 1825-1828, transferred to New York as general agent for the Mississippi Valley 1828-1832, and became finance secretary in 1832 — the role he held into the Civil War era. Buried Woodlawn Cemetery Bronx NY where his son John and daughter-in-law Lucy Ashmun would later join him.

Born Amherst MA 1796. Yale College B.A. 1821 followed by Andover Theological Seminary 1824 — the standard New England Congregationalist pipeline of the 1820s. After Andover served as agent for the ABCFM (American Board of Commissioners for Foreign Missions) before joining the newly-founded American Tract Society in 1825.

Born Amherst MA 1796. Yale College B.A. 1821 followed by Andover Theological Seminary 1824 — the standard New England Congregationalist pipeline of the 1820s. After Andover served as agent for the ABCFM (American Board of Commissioners for Foreign Missions) before joining the newly-founded American Tract Society in 1825.

birth.notes: EH gives 'born at Hartford CT' (father Henry was cashier of Phoenix Bank Hartford 1836-1847); FindAGrave inconsistently gives Westfield. EH Hartford preferred — father was working there.

marriages.notes: EH gives Sep 10, 1879 marriage; FamilySearch records Sep 4 1879 Westfield MA — minor discrepancy.

narrative_blocks.header: Lost two infant sons in their first months
narrative_blocks.body: Of her three children with John Cotton Eastman of New York, the first two — Paul (1880) and Henry Hooker (1881-1882) — both died as infants. Only daughter Elizabeth Reed, born 1886, survived to adulthood. Lucy and John lived at New Haven CT before retiring to Concord NH, where both died in 1914 and lie together at Woodlawn Cemetery in the Bronx.

INST022.primary_name: American Board of Commissioners for Foreign Missions
INST022.name: American Board of Commissioners for Foreign Missions
INST022.notes: First American foreign-mission organization. Tree connections: Rev. Elias Cornelius (agent 1816, secretary 1831-32); Ainsworth and Harriet Ellsworth Blunt, missionaries to the Cherokee at Brainerd, Tennessee; Eliza Talcott, missionary to Japan; Rev. William Banfield Capron, Madura mission; Judge John Hooker, member.

INST137.name: Andover Theological Seminary
alt_names: Andover Newton Theological School (post-1908 merger with Newton Theological Institution)
INST137.notes: Founded 1807 as a conservative Trinitarian Congregational response to Unitarian Harvard. The most prestigious Protestant theological seminary in antebellum America. Trained ministers including Rev. John Ripley Adams (X02847, 1826), Rev. Edward Strong Dwight (HD1983), and many ABCFM missionaries. Merged with Newton Theological Institution 1908 to form Andover Newton Theological School; relocated to Newton MA. Now affiliated with Yale Divinity School (as of 2017). | Merged from INST050: Founded 1808 in Andover MA. Leading Calvinist seminary. Notable Hooker-tree connection: Rev. John Erskine Edwards (HD2631) graduated 1835.

INST041.primary_name: American Board of Commissioners for Foreign Missions (ABCFM)
INST041.name: American Board of Commissioners for Foreign Missions (ABCFM)
INST041.notes: Founded 1810 at Andover Seminary, Massachusetts by prominent Congregationalists. First American foreign missionary society; first missions to China and Ceylon. Yale President Rev. Timothy Dwight IV (H00914) was among the founding figures; Dwight Mission (established 1820 in present-day Arkansas/Oklahoma for the Cherokee) was named in his honor. The board operated Brainerd Mission (Chattanooga TN, est.1817) where Harriet Ellsworth Blunt (X02121) and Ainsworth Blunt (X02122) served the Cherokee. HOOKER TREE CONNECTIONS: 1. Rev. Timothy Dwight IV (H00914) — Yale President 1795-1817; founding ABCFM figure; Dwight Mission named for him. 5th-generation Hooker descendant. 2. Elias Cornelius (X00724) — ABCFM Secretary of Domestic Correspondence 1819-1832; conducted 1817 missionary tour to Creek and Cherokee; m. Mary Ann Hooker (H01355). 3. Rev. Edward William Hooker (H01354) — corporate ABCFM member from 1840. 4. Harriet Ellsworth Blunt (X02121) — served at Brainerd Mission 1821-1837; granddaughter of Rev. Cyprian Strong (H00299). 5. Ainsworth Blunt (X02122) — Brainerd missionary 1822-1837; accompanied Cherokee on Trail of Tears 1838; first mayor of Dalton GA. 6. Dr. Mary Floyd Cushman (HD2818, gen 8) — ABCFM medical missionary in Angola 1922-1953. 7. Samuel Gelston Dwight (HD2376) — ABCFM missionary to Hawaii; children named for ABCFM colleagues Juliette Cooke and Samuel Castle.

INST140.name: American Tract Society
INST140.notes:
Nonprofit nonsectarian evangelical organization founded May 11, 1825 New York City for the purpose of publishing and disseminating Christian tracts. Lineage traces back through the New York Tract Society (1812) and the New England Tract Society (1814) to the Religious Tract Society of London (1799). Theologically conservative; board-governed with an advisory Council of Reference. Headquartered New York City 1825-1978; relocated to Garland TX 1978. Joint publishing agreement with Good News Publishing (Crossway division) 2012.

Hooker-tree connection: Rev. Ornan Eastman (X02862) joined ATS at its 1825 founding — served at the Boston office 1825-1828, then as general agent for the Mississippi Valley out of NY 1828-1832, then as finance secretary from 1832 into the Civil War era. His son John Cotton Eastman (I01132) married Lucy Ashmun Hooker (H04364) in 1879.
